policyName: Specialty Pharmacy: Dupixent (dupilumab) for Atopic Dermatitis

criteria.description: Confirmed diagnosis of moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis by dermatologist (IGA score >= 3 or EASI score >= 16 or BSA >= 10%)
Failed or contraindicated to at least ONE topical corticosteroid (medium or high potency) used for minimum 4 weeks
Failed or contraindicated to at least ONE topical calcineurin inhibitor (tacrolimus or pimecrolimus) used for minimum 4 weeks
Failed, intolerant, or contraindicated to at least ONE systemic immunosuppressant (methotrexate, cyclosporine, azathioprine, or mycophenolate) used for minimum 3 months
Prescribing physician is a dermatologist or allergist/immunologist
Patient is age 6 years or older
No active systemic infection and TB screening completed

requirements: Documented clinical improvement (reduction in IGA, EASI, or BSA)
No significant adverse effects requiring discontinuation

urgentBypass: Severe flare with erythroderma or hospitalization — expedited 24-hour review.
denialReasons: Step therapy not completed (topical steroids, calcineurin inhibitors, or systemic agents not tried)
Mild atopic dermatitis (IGA < 3 and EASI < 16 and BSA < 10%)
Prescriber is not dermatologist or allergist
Active systemic infection or untreated latent TB
